children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: An order shall contain the terms and provisions specified in this section.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: The order shall provide for the establishment of a Fresh Cut Flowers and Fresh Cut Greens Promotion Council, consisting of 25 members, to administer the order.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: i Appointment
children.text: The order shall provide that members of the PromoFlor Council shall be appointed by the Secretary from nominations submitted as provided in paragraphs (2) and (3).

children.label: I
children.text: participating qualified handlers representing qualified wholesale handlers and producers and importers that are qualified handlers;

children.label: II
children.text: representatives of traditional retailers; and

children.label: III
children.text: representatives of persons who produce fresh cut flowers and fresh cut greens.

children.label: i
children.text: 14 members representing qualified wholesale handlers of domestic or imported cut flowers and cut greens;

children.label: ii
children.text: 3 members representing producers that are qualified handlers of cut flowers and cut greens;

children.label: iii
children.text: 3 members representing importers that are qualified handlers of cut flowers and cut greens;

children.label: iv
children.text: 3 members representing traditional cut flowers and cut greens retailers; and

children.label: I
children.text: 1 member shall represent persons who produce the flowers or greens in locations that are east of the Mississippi River; and

children.label: II
children.text: 1 member shall represent persons who produce the flowers or greens in locations that are west of the Mississippi River.

children.heading: Importer that is a qualified handler
children.label: i Importer that is a qualified handler
I
children.text: whose principal activity is the importation of cut flowers or cut greens into the United States (either directly or as an agent, broker, or consignee of any person or nation that produces or handles cut flowers or cut greens outside the United States for sale in the United States); and

children.label: II
children.text: that is subject to assessments as a qualified handler under the order.

children.heading: Producer that is a qualified handler
children.label: ii Producer that is a qualified handler
aa
children.text: in the domestic production, for sale in commerce, of cut flowers or cut greens and that owns or shares in the ownership and risk of loss of the cut flowers or cut greens; or

children.label: bb
children.text: as a first processor of noncultivated cut greens, in receiving the cut greens from a person who gathers the cut greens for handling; and

children.label: II
children.text: is subject to assessments as a qualified handler under the order.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The term “qualified wholesale handler” means a person in business as a floral wholesale jobber or floral supplier that is subject to assessments as a qualified handler under the order.

children.heading: Floral supplier
children.label: aa Floral supplier
children.text: The term “floral supplier” means a person engaged in acquiring cut flowers or cut greens to be manufactured into floral articles or otherwise processed for resale.

children.heading: Floral wholesale jobber
children.label: bb Floral wholesale jobber
children.text: The term “floral wholesale jobber” means a person who conducts a commission or other wholesale business in buying and selling cut flowers or cut greens.

children.heading: Distribution of qualified wholesale handler appointments
children.label: C Distribution of qualified wholesale handler appointments
children.text: The order shall provide that the appointments of qualified wholesale handlers to the PromoFlor Council made by the Secretary shall take into account the geographical distribution of cut flowers and cut greens markets in the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: 2 nominees shall be submitted for each appointment to the PromoFlor Council;

children.label: B
children.text: nominations for each appointment of a qualified wholesale handler, producer that is a qualified handler, or importer that is a qualified handler to the PromoFlor Council shall be made by qualified wholesale handlers, producers that are qualified handlers, or importers that are qualified handlers, respectively, through an election process, in accordance with regulations issued by the Secretary;

children.label: i
children.text: 1 of the retailer appointments shall be made by the American Floral Marketing Council or a successor entity; and

children.label: ii
children.text: 2 of the retailer appointments shall be made by traditional retail florist organizations, in accordance with regulations issued by the Secretary;

children.label: D
children.text: nominations for each appointment of a representative of persons who produce fresh cut flowers and fresh cut greens shall be made by the persons through an election process, in accordance with regulations issued by the Secretary; and

children.label: E
children.text: in any case in which qualified wholesale handlers, producers that are qualified handlers, importers that are qualified handlers, persons who produce fresh cut flowers and fresh cut greens, or retailers fail to nominate individuals for an appointment to the PromoFlor Council, the Secretary may appoint an individual to fill the vacancy on a basis provided in the order or other regulations of the Secretary.

children.heading: Alternates
children.label: 4 Alternates
children.text: The order shall provide for the selection of alternate members of the PromoFlor Council by the Secretary in accordance with procedures specified in the order.

children.label: A
children.text: each term of appointment to the PromoFlor Council shall be for 3 years, except that, of the initial appointments, 9 of the appointments shall be for 2-year terms, 8 of the appointments shall be for 3-year terms, and 8 of the appointments shall be for 4-year terms;

children.label: B
children.text: no member of the PromoFlor Council may serve more than 2 consecutive terms of 3 years, except that any member serving an initial term of 4 years may serve an additional term of 3 years; and

children.label: C
children.text: members of the PromoFlor Council shall serve without compensation, but shall be reimbursed for the expenses of the members incurred in performing duties as members of the PromoFlor Council.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The order shall authorize the PromoFlor Council to appoint, from among the members of the Council, an executive committee of not more than 9 members.

children.label: I
children.text: 4 members representing qualified wholesale handlers;

children.label: II
children.text: 2 members representing producers that are qualified handlers;

children.label: III
children.text: 2 members representing importers that are qualified handlers; and

children.label: IV
children.text: 1 member representing traditional retailers.

children.heading: Subsequent membership
children.label: iii Subsequent membership
children.text: After the initial appointments, each appointment to the executive committee shall be made so as to ensure that the committee reflects, to the maximum extent practicable, the membership composition of the PromoFlor Council as a whole.

children.heading: Terms
children.label: iv Terms
children.text: Each initial appointment to the executive committee shall be for a term of 2 years. After the initial appointments, each appointment to the executive committee shall be for a term of 1 year.

children.heading: Authority
children.label: B Authority
children.text: The PromoFlor Council may delegate to the executive committee the authority of the PromoFlor Council under the order to hire and manage staff and conduct the routine business of the PromoFlor Council consistent with such policies as are determined by the PromoFlor Council.

children.heading: General responsibilities of PromoFlor Council
children.label: c General responsibilities of PromoFlor Council

children.text: administer the order in accordance with the terms and provisions of the order;
make rules and regulations to effectuate the terms and provisions of the order;
appoint members of the PromoFlor Council to serve on an executive committee;
employ such persons as the PromoFlor Council determines are necessary, and set the compensation and define the duties of the persons;

children.label: A
children.text: develop budgets for the implementation of the order and submit the budgets to the Secretary for approval under subsection (d); and

children.label: B
children.text: propose and develop (or receive and evaluate), approve, and submit to the Secretary for approval under subsection (d) plans and projects for cut flowers or cut greens promotion, consumer information, or related research;

children.label: A
children.text: implement plans and projects for cut flowers or cut greens promotion, consumer information, or related research, as provided in subsection (d); or

children.label: B
children.text: contract or enter into agreements with appropriate persons to implement the plans and projects, as provided in subsection (e), and pay the costs of the implementation, or contracts and agreements, with funds received under the order;
evaluate on-going and completed plans and projects for cut flowers or cut greens promotion, consumer information, or related research;
receive, investigate, and report to the Secretary complaints of violations of the order;
recommend to the Secretary amendments to the order;

children.label: A
children.text: obligations of the United States or any agency of the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: general obligations of any State or any political subdivision of a State;

children.label: C
children.text: any interest-bearing account or certificate of deposit of a bank that is a member of the Federal Reserve System; or

children.label: D
children.text: obligations fully guaranteed as to principal and interest by the United States,
provide the Secretary such information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Submission of budgets
children.label: 1 Submission of budgets
children.text: The order shall require the PromoFlor Council to submit to the Secretary for approval budgets, on a fiscal year basis, of the anticipated expenses and disbursements of the Council in the implementation of the order, including the projected costs of cut flowers and cut greens promotion, consumer information, and related research plans and projects.

children.label: i
children.text: for the establishment, implementation, administration, and evaluation of appropriate plans and projects for advertising, sales promotion, other promotion, and consumer information with respect to cut flowers and cut greens, and for the disbursement of necessary funds for the purposes described in this clause;

children.label: ii
children.text: that any plan or project referred to in clause (i) shall be directed toward increasing the general demand for cut flowers or cut greens and may not make reference to a private brand or trade name, point of origin, or source of supply, except that this clause shall not preclude the PromoFlor Council from offering the plans and projects of the Council for use by commercial parties, under terms and conditions prescribed by the PromoFlor Council and approved by the Secretary; and

children.label: iii
children.text: that no plan or project may make use of unfair or deceptive acts or practices with respect to quality or value.

children.label: I
children.text: market development research;

children.label: II
children.text: research with respect to the sale, distribution, marketing, or use of cut flowers or cut greens; and

children.label: III
children.text: other research with respect to cut flowers or cut greens marketing, promotion, or consumer information;

children.label: ii
children.text: the dissemination of the information acquired through the plans and projects; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the disbursement of such funds as are necessary to carry out this subparagraph.

children.heading: Submission to Secretary
children.label: C Submission to Secretary
children.text: The order shall provide that the PromoFlor Council shall submit to the Secretary for approval a proposed plan or project for cut flowers or cut greens promotion, consumer information, or related research, as described in subparagraphs (A) and (B).

children.heading: Approval by Secretary
children.label: 3 Approval by Secretary
children.text: A budget, or plan or project for cut flowers or cut greens promotion, consumer information, or related research may not be implemented prior to approval of the budget, plan, or project by the Secretary.

children.heading: Promotion, consumer information, and related research plans and projects
children.label: 1 Promotion, consumer information, and related research plans and projects

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: To ensure efficient use of funds, the order shall provide that the PromoFlor Council, with the approval of the Secretary, may enter into a contract or an agreement for the implementation of a plan or project for promotion, consumer information, or related research with respect to cut flowers or cut greens, and for the payment of the cost of the contract or agreement with funds received by the PromoFlor Council under the order.

children.label: i
children.text: the contracting or agreeing party shall develop and submit to the PromoFlor Council a plan or project, together with a budget that includes the estimated costs to be incurred for the plan or project;

children.label: ii
children.text: the plan or project shall become effective on the approval of the Secretary; and

children.label: I
children.text: keep accurate records of all of the transactions of the party;

children.label: II
children.text: account for funds received and expended;

children.label: III
children.text: make periodic reports to the PromoFlor Council of activities conducted; and

children.label: IV
children.text: make such other reports as the PromoFlor Council or the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Other contracts and agreements
children.label: 2 Other contracts and agreements
children.text: The order shall provide that the PromoFlor Council may enter into a contract or agreement for administrative services. Any contract or agreement entered into under this paragraph shall include provisions comparable to the provisions described in paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: Books and records of PromoFlor Council
children.label: f Books and records of PromoFlor Council
A
children.text: maintain such books and records (which shall be available to the Secretary for inspection and audit) as the Secretary may require;

children.label: B
children.text: prepare and submit to the Secretary, from time to time, such reports as the Secretary may require; and

children.label: C
children.text: account for the receipt and disbursement of all funds entrusted to the PromoFlor Council.

children.heading: Audits
children.label: 2 Audits
children.text: The PromoFlor Council shall cause the books and records of the Council to be audited by an independent auditor at the end of each fiscal year. A report of each audit shall be submitted to the Secretary.

children.heading: Control of administrative costs
children.label: g Control of administrative costs
children.text: The order shall provide that the PromoFlor Council shall, as soon as practicable after the order becomes effective and after consultation with the Secretary and other appropriate persons, implement a system of cost controls based on normally accepted business practices that will ensure that the annual budgets of the PromoFlor Council include only amounts for administrative expenses that cover the minimum administrative activities and personnel needed to properly administer and enforce the order, and conduct, supervise, and evaluate plans and projects under the order.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The order shall provide that each qualified handler shall pay to the PromoFlor Council, in the manner provided in the order, an assessment on each sale of cut flowers or cut greens to a retailer or an exempt handler (including each transaction described in subparagraph (C)(ii)), except to the extent that the sale is excluded from assessments under .section 6805(a) of this title

children.heading: Published lists
children.label: B Published lists
children.text: To facilitate the payment of assessments under this paragraph, the PromoFlor Council shall publish lists of qualified handlers required to pay assessments under the order and exempt handlers.

children.heading: Qualified handler status
children.label: i Qualified handler status
children.text: The order shall contain provisions regarding the determination of the status of a person as a qualified handler or exempt handler that include the rules and requirements specified in sections 6802(4) and 6805(b) of this title.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The order shall provide that each non-sale transfer of cut flowers or cut greens to a retailer from a qualified handler that is a distribution center (as described in ), and each direct sale of cut flowers or cut greens to a consumer by a qualified handler that is an importer or a producer (as described in ), shall be treated as a sale of cut flowers or cut greens to a retailer subject to assessments under this subsection.section 6802(4)(A)(ii)(II) of this titlesection 6802(4)(A)(iii) of this title

children.heading: Amount of sale in the case of non-sale transfers and direct sales by importers
children.label: II Amount of sale in the case of non-sale transfers and direct sales by importers
aa
children.text: the price paid by the distribution center or importer, respectively, to acquire the cut flowers or cut greens; and

children.label: bb
children.text: an amount determined by multiplying the acquisition price referred to in item (aa) by a uniform percentage established by the order to represent the mark-up of a wholesale handler on a sale to a retailer.

children.heading: Direct sales by producers
children.label: III Direct sales by producers
children.text: Subject to subclause (IV), in the case of a direct sale to a consumer by a producer, the amount of the sale shall be equal to an amount determined by multiplying the price paid by the consumer by a uniform percentage established by the order to represent the cost of producing the article and the mark-up of a wholesale handler on a sale to a retailer.

children.label: aa
children.text: recommendation by the PromoFlor Council; and

children.label: bb
children.text: approval by the Secretary after public notice and opportunity for comment in accordance with and without regard to sections 556 and 557 of such title.section 553 of title 5

children.label: i
children.text: the gross sales price of the cut flowers or cut greens sold; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of transactions described in paragraph (1)(C)(ii), the amount of each transaction calculated as provided in paragraph (1)(C)(ii).

children.label: I
children.text: the gross sales price of a product sold; or

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of transactions described in paragraph (1)(C)(ii), the amount of each transaction calculated as provided in paragraph (1)(C)(ii),

children.label: I
children.text: may be made only if adopted by the PromoFlor Council by at least a ⅔ majority vote and approved by the Secretary as necessary to achieve the objectives of this chapter (after public notice and opportunity for comment in accordance with and without regard to sections 556 and 557 of such title);section 553 of title 5

children.label: II
children.text: shall be announced by the PromoFlor Council not less than 30 days prior to going into effect; and

children.label: III
children.text: shall not be subject to a vote in a referendum conducted under .section 6806 of this title

children.heading: Timing of submitting assessments
children.label: 3 Timing of submitting assessments
children.text: The order shall provide that each person required to pay assessments under this subsection shall remit, to the PromoFlor Council, the assessment due from each sale by the person of cut flowers or cut greens that is subject to an assessment within such time period after the sale (not to exceed 60 days after the end of the month in which the sale took place) as is specified in the order.

children.label: i
children.text: establish an escrow account to be used for assessment refunds, as needed; and

children.label: ii
children.text: place into the account an amount equal to 10 percent of the total amount of assessments collected during the period beginning on the date the order becomes effective, as provided in , and ending on the date the initial referendum on the order under is completed.section 6803(b)(3)(B) of this titlesection 6806(a) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the qualified handler is required to pay the assessments;

children.label: II
children.text: the qualified handler does not support the program established under this chapter;

children.label: III
children.text: the qualified handler demands the refund prior to the conduct of the referendum on the order under ; andsection 6806(a) of this title

children.label: IV
children.text: the order is not approved by qualified handlers in the referendum.

children.label: I
children.text: The demand shall be made in accordance with regulations, on a form, and within a time period specified by the PromoFlor Council.

children.label: II
children.text: The refund shall be made only on submission of proof satisfactory to the PromoFlor Council that the qualified handler paid the assessment for which the refund is demanded.

children.label: III
children.text: If the amount in the escrow account required under subparagraph (A) is not sufficient to refund the total amount of assessments demanded by all qualified handlers determined eligible for refunds and the order is not approved in the referendum on the order under , the PromoFlor Council shall prorate the amount of all such refunds among all eligible qualified handlers that demand the refund.section 6806(a) of this title

children.heading: Program approved
children.label: C Program approved
children.text: The order shall provide that, if the order is approved in the referendum conducted under , there shall be no refunds made, and all funds in the escrow account shall be returned to the PromoFlor Council for use by the PromoFlor Council in accordance with the other provisions of the order.section 6806(a) of this title

children.heading: Use of assessment funds
children.label: 5 Use of assessment funds
children.text: The order shall provide that assessment funds (less any refunds expended under the terms of the order required under paragraph (4)) shall be used for payment of costs incurred in implementing and administering the order, with provision for a reasonable reserve, and to cover the administrative costs incurred by the Secretary in implementing and administering this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to the other provisions of this paragraph and notwithstanding any other provision of this chapter, the PromoFlor Council may grant a postponement of the payment of an assessment under this subsection for any qualified handler that establishes that the handler is financially unable to make the payment.

children.heading: Requirements and procedures
children.label: ii Requirements and procedures
children.text: A handler described in clause (i) shall establish that the handler is financially unable to make the payment in accordance with application and documentation requirements and review procedures established under rules recommended by the PromoFlor Council, approved by the Secretary, and issued after public notice and opportunity for comment in accordance with and without regard to sections 556 and 557 of such title.section 553 of title 5

children.heading: Criteria and responsibility for determinations
children.label: B Criteria and responsibility for determinations
children.text: The PromoFlor Council may grant a postponement under subparagraph (A) only if the handler demonstrates by the submission of an opinion of an independent certified public accountant, and by submission of other documentation required under the rules established under subparagraph (A)(ii), that the handler is insolvent or will be unable to continue to operate if the handler is required to pay the assessment when otherwise due.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The time period of a postponement and the terms and conditions of the payment of each assessment that is postponed under this paragraph shall be established by the PromoFlor Council, in accordance with rules established under the procedures specified in subparagraph (A)(ii), so as to appropriately reflect the demonstrated needs of the qualified handler.

children.heading: Extensions
children.label: ii Extensions
children.text: A postponement may be extended under rules established under the procedures specified in subparagraph (A)(ii) for the grant of initial postponements.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: i Prohibition
children.text: The order shall prohibit the use of any funds received by the PromoFlor Council in any manner for the purpose of influencing legislation or government action or policy, except that the funds may be used by the PromoFlor Council for the development and recommendation to the Secretary of amendments to the order.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The order shall provide that each qualified handler shall maintain, and make available for inspection, such books and records as are required by the order and file reports at the time, in the manner, and having the content required by the order, to the end that such information is made available to the Secretary and the PromoFlor Council as is appropriate for the administration or enforcement of this chapter, the order, or any regulation issued under this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Information obtained from books, records, or reports under paragraph (1) or subsection (h)(6), or from reports required under , shall be kept confidential by all officers and employees of the Department of Agriculture and by the staff and agents of the PromoFlor Council.section 6805(b)(3) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: in a suit or administrative hearing brought at the request of the Secretary, or to which the Secretary or any officer of the United States is a party, involving the order; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to the extent the Secretary considers the information relevant to the suit or hearing.

children.label: i
children.text: the issuance of general statements, based on the reports, of the number of persons subject to the order or statistical data collected from the reports, if the statements do not identify the information furnished by any person; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the publication, by direction of the Secretary, of the name of any person who violates the order, together with a statement of the particular provisions of the order violated by the person.

children.heading: Review
children.label: A Review
children.text: The order shall provide that the staff of the PromoFlor Council shall periodically review lists of importers of cut flowers and cut greens to determine whether persons on the lists are subject to the order.

children.heading: Customs service
children.label: B Customs service
children.text: On the request of the PromoFlor Council, the Commissioner of the United States Customs Service shall provide to the PromoFlor Council lists of importers of cut flowers and cut greens.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The order shall provide that the PromoFlor Council, from time to time, may seek advice from and consult with experts from the production, import, wholesale, and retail segments of the cut flowers and cut greens industry to assist in the development of promotion, consumer information, and related research plans and projects.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For the purposes described in paragraph (1), the order shall authorize the appointment of special committees composed of persons other than PromoFlor Council members.

children.label: i
children.text: may not provide advice or recommendations to a representative of an agency, or an officer, of the Federal Government; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall consult directly with the PromoFlor Council.

children.heading: Other terms of order
children.label: l Other terms of order
children.text: The order shall contain such other terms and provisions, consistent with this chapter, as are necessary to carry out this chapter (including provision for the assessment of interest and a charge for each late payment of assessments under subsection (h) and for carrying out ).section 6805 of this title
